title: 10 best herbal teas for asthma
intro_desc: Herbal teas for asthma are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate asthma symptoms. These teas contain natural anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator properties, which help relax the airways and improve breathing. Some popular herbal teas for asthma include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, turmeric, and fenugreek. Chamomile tea, for instance, contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation, while ginger tea has been shown to improve lung function. Licorice root tea can help soothe irritated airways, and turmeric tea contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and relieves asthma symptoms. Fenugreek tea, on the other hand, can help reduce asthma-related coughing and wheezing. Drinking these teas regularly can significantly improve the quality of life for asthma sufferers by reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European goldenrod tea helps with asthma because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing. The tea's flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, can help relax bronchial muscles, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, goldenrod tea has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help clear mucus from the respiratory tract and alleviate asthma symptoms. Its soothing and calming effects can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with asthma attacks. Overall, European goldenrod tea can be a natural and effective way to manage asthma symptoms and improve respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: European goldenrod tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Bronchodilator: The tea acts as a bronchodilator, helping to widen the airways and improve airflow to the lungs, which is crucial for asthma management. This effect is attributed to the presence of volatile oils and saponins.

Antioxidant: European goldenrod tea contains antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the risk of asthma attacks. The high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids are responsible for its antioxidant properties.

Antispasmodic: The tea has antispasmodic properties that help relax the smooth muscles in the airways, preventing asthma-related spasms and coughing fits. This is due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: European goldenrod tea supports the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and reducing the likelihood of asthma exacerbations. This is attributed to the presence of various essential oils, polysaccharides, and vitamins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, flowers of European goldenrod are used in teas to reduce inflammation and help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, goldenrod leaves are often used in teas to help clear airways and soothe respiratory ailments like asthma.
Buds: Containing essential oils, goldenrod buds can help relax bronchial spasms and relieve asthma-related congestion when brewed in a tea.
Stems: With some anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, goldenrod stems can help support respiratory health and offer mild relief for asthma symptoms in tea form.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes until it reaches a temperature of around 200 degrees Fahrenheit.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried European goldenrod herb to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to be released.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the herb.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to help manage asthma symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects. These properties can help to relax the airways, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of asthma such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, thyme tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking thyme tea regularly may also help to boost the immune system and reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. However, it is important to note that thyme tea should not be used as a substitute for prescribed asthma medication and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Bronchodilator: Thyme tea helps to widen the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe by relaxing the muscles in the bronchioles. This property is important for asthma because it alleviates the constriction of the airways that occurs during asthma attacks. The active constituents responsible for this effect are volatile oils such as carvacrol and thymol.

Expectorant: Thyme tea aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and inflammation. This property is crucial for asthma as it helps clear the airways and reduces the likelihood of asthma attacks triggered by mucus buildup. The expectorant effect is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, thus alleviating asthma symptoms. This property is significant for asthma because inflammation is a key factor in asthma pathogenesis. The anti-inflammatory effects are primarily due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antispasmodic: Thyme tea helps relax the smooth muscles in the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. This property is vital for asthma because spasms in the bronchioles can lead to asthma symptoms or exacerbations. The active constituents responsible for this effect are volatile oils like carvacrol and thymol.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains antioxidants that protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage. This property is essential for asthma as oxidative stress contributes to the development and progression of asthma. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-spasmodic properties that help relax the muscles of the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help clear phlegm and reduce inflammation in the airways.
Stems: Thyme stems contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways and protect against oxidative stress.
Seeds: Thyme seeds are rich in thymol, a compound with antiseptic and expectorant properties, which help clear phlegm and soothe coughs.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves or 4 teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves, known for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Place the thyme leaves in a tea infuser or heat-resistant container.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the thyme leaves, allowing them to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
After steeping, remove the tea infuser or strain the liquid to discard the thyme leaves.
Drink the thyme tea while warm, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help manage asthma symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with asthma because it contains anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help improve respiratory function. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The leaves contain compounds such as eucalyptol, which has been shown to help relax the airways and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. This can help alleviate symptoms of asthma such as shortness of breath, coughing, and wheezing. Additionally, the tea may also help boost the immune system, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma. This is due to the presence of terpinen-4-ol and α-pinene.

Bronchodilator: The tea acts as a bronchodilator, helping to widen the airways and improve airflow to the lungs, which is crucial for asthma management. This effect is attributed to the presence of 1,8-cineole.

Antispasmodic: Tasmanian blue gum tea has antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the airways, reducing the likelihood of asthma attacks. This is due to the presence of methyl eugenol.

Expectorant: The tea acts as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier for asthma sufferers to breathe. This is attributed to the presence of α-pinene and limonene.

Antioxidant: Tasmanian blue gum tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and contain compounds that help relax bronchial muscles, making it easier to breathe.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce airway inflammation, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Bark: Has been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, as it contains compounds that may help dilate the bronchioles and improve breathing.
Seeds: High in essential oils, which can help reduce inflammation and ease asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 4 cups of water.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves to a tea infuser or reusable tea bag.
Once the water has boiled, pour it over the tea-infused leaves in a teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea leaves and enjoy the herbal tea, preferably between meals for best asthma relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena tea helps with asthma because it contains a variety of compounds, such as flavonoids and verbascoside, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, verbena tea has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscles, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Furthermore, verbena tea is caffeine-free, making it a safe and natural alternative to traditional asthma medications. However, it is important to note that while verbena tea may help manage asthma symptoms, it should not be used as a substitute for prescribed asthma medications or treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Verbena tea contains verbascoside, which helps reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Bronchodilator: Verbena tea has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscles, improving airflow and making it easier to breathe for asthma sufferers.
Antispasmodic: The tea's antispasmodic properties, attributed to its rosmarinic acid content, help relieve muscle spasms in the respiratory tract and reduce coughing fits.
Antioxidant: Verbena tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, both of which are important for managing asthma.

Immune system support: Verbena tea can help boost the immune system, thanks to its vitamin C and other antioxidant content, helping asthma patients better fight off infections that could exacerbate their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, verbena leaves can help reduce airway inflammation and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and essential oils, verbena flowers can help relax bronchial spasms and ease breathing in asthma patients.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients and active compounds, verbena seeds can help support respiratory health and provide relief from asthma-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried lemon verbena leaves to the boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, for asthma relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with asthma because it contains mucilage, a slippery substance that coats the throat and soothes irritation. This can help reduce coughing and make it easier to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing. The tea is also a natural expectorant, which helps to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs. All of these properties make marshmallow tea a helpful natural remedy for asthma symptoms. However, it is important to note that marshmallow tea should not be used as a substitute for prescribed asthma medication and should be used in conjunction with a doctor's advice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma. This is due to the mucilage content, which forms a protective layer on the mucous membranes.

Expectorant: Marshmallow tea acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating asthma symptoms. The mucilage content is responsible for this effect, as it can soothe irritated membranes and help eliminate phlegm.

Antitussive: Marshmallow tea has antitussive properties, helping to suppress coughs associated with asthma. This is attributed to the tea's demulcent and soothing effects on the respiratory tract, reducing the urge to cough.

Soothing: Marshmallow tea has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes lining the respiratory tract, providing relief from asthma symptoms. This is due to the high mucilage content, which forms a protective layer on the membranes and reduces irritation.

Immune-boosting: Marshmallow tea has immune-boosting properties that can help strengthen the body's defenses against asthma triggers. This is attributed to the tea's antioxidant content, which can help protect cells from damage and support overall immune function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Flowers: Containing soothing mucilage, flowers help coat and protect the respiratory tract, providing relief from asthma-related coughing and irritation.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants, seeds aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, supporting overall lung health and asthma management.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of mixed dried herbs (such as licorice root, thyme, and chamomile) suitable for asthma relief.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid plant material.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with asthma because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms. Additionally, licorice tea can help soothe irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Furthermore, licorice tea can help loosen phlegm and mucus, making it easier to expel and clear the airways. Drinking licorice tea can also help relax the bronchial tubes, reducing spasms and constriction in the airways. All of these properties make licorice tea a potentially helpful natural remedy for asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe, due to its active constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
Expectorant: Licorice tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and making breathing easier, thanks to its expectorant properties.

Immune-boosting: Licorice tea can help strengthen the immune system, which is important for managing asthma since asthma attacks can be triggered by viral or bacterial infections, due to its active constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
Antispasmodic: Licorice tea can help relax the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks, due to its antispasmodic properties.

Antioxidant: Licorice tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the development and progression of asthma, due to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation in the airways, making it useful for treating asthma symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain coumarins, which have a relaxing effect on the bronchioles and can help reduce asthma symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tea plant contain various flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the licorice root.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe asthma symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coltsfoot tea helps with asthma because it contains natural anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator properties that can help open up the airways and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The herb's mucilage content also helps soothe irritated membranes in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, coltsfoot tea has been shown to stimulate the production of surfactant, a substance that helps keep the airways open and lubricated, further alleviating asthma symptoms. Overall, coltsfoot tea can be a natural and effective way to manage asthma symptoms and promote respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Coltsfoot tea helps clear mucus and reduce congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for those with asthma. This is due to the presence of mucilage and flavonoids in the plant.
Anti-inflammatory: Coltsfoot tea reduces inflammation in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms. This is attributed to the anti-inflammatory properties of the plant's flavonoids and essential oils.
Bronchodilator: Coltsfoot tea helps relax and widen the airways, improving airflow to the lungs. This effect is attributed to the alkaloids present in the plant, such as senkirkine.

Antispasmodic: Coltsfoot tea reduces muscle spasms in the respiratory tract, alleviating coughing and shortness of breath. This is due to the plant's antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids.

Antioxidant: Coltsfoot tea contains antioxidants that protect the lungs from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation. This can help prevent asthma attacks and reduce their severity. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of coltsfoot tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating asthma symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of coltsfoot tea plant contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the respiratory tract, providing relief from asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of coltsfoot tea plant contain expectorant and bronchodilator properties, which help to clear mucus from the airways and improve breathing for those with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried coltsfoot herb.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the coltsfoot herb.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles.
Drink 2-3 cups of coltsfoot tea daily, between meals, for asthma relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with asthma because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin and luteolin. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the tea has been shown to help relax bronchial muscles, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. Furthermore, it may boost the immune system, which can help protect against asthma triggers like viral infections. Drinking eastern purple coneflower tea may provide a natural and effective way to manage asthma symptoms and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma. This is due to the presence of active constituents like chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.
Bronchodilator: The tea can help relax and widen the airways, improving airflow and reducing asthma symptoms, thanks to the presence of alkamides.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains antioxidants that help protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, contributing to overall lung health and asthma management. The antioxidant properties come from compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Immune-boosting: The tea can help strengthen the immune system, which is important for asthma management as it often involves an overactive immune response. Active constituents like polysaccharides and essential oils contribute to this immune-boosting effect.

Antispasmodic: Eastern purple coneflower tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relieve muscle spasms in the airways, reducing coughing and difficulty breathing associated with asthma. This is due to the presence of alkaloids and saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the flowers help reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Leaves: High in essential oils and active compounds, the leaves have anti-spasmodic and bronchodilator properties, making them suitable for asthma relief.
Seeds: Packed with beneficial nutrients, the seeds can help soothe respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and strengthen the immune system, thus benefiting asthma patients.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower herb, also known as Echinacea purpurea.
Steep the herb in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to extract its medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the used herb and ensure a smooth drink.
Add honey or lemon to taste, as desired for sweetness or tanginess.
Consume the tea while warm, up to three times a day, for asthma relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop tea helps with asthma because it contains natural anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator properties that can help open up airways and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work together to soothe irritation and alleviate symptoms of asthma, including coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, hyssop tea has been shown to help relax the muscles in the airways, further improving breathing and promoting overall lung health. By drinking hyssop tea regularly, individuals with asthma may be able to reduce the frequency and severity of their symptoms and improve their quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Hyssop tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Expectorant: Hyssop tea aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear airways and alleviate asthma symptoms. This is attributed to the essential oil content, particularly pinocamphone and camphene.
Bronchodilator: Hyssop tea helps relax bronchial smooth muscles, widening the airways and improving breathing for asthma sufferers. This is facilitated by the marrubiin and pinocamphone content.
Antispasmodic: Hyssop tea alleviates muscle spasms, reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. This is due to the presence of marrubiin and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Hyssop tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses, helping prevent respiratory infections that can trigger asthma symptoms. This is attributed to the essential oil content, particularly 1,8-cineole and pinocamphone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop tea plant contain essential oils and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relaxing the muscles of the respiratory system and alleviating asthma symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of the hyssop tea plant have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe for those with asthma.
Stems: The stems of the hyssop tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-asthmatic effects, reducing the severity of asthma attacks and improving lung function.
Seeds: The seeds of the hyssop tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to widen the airways and improve breathing for those with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried hyssop leaves to a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the hyssop leaves and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and discard the used hyssop leaves.
Drink the hyssop tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet basil tea helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. These properties can help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, sweet basil tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. The tea may also help to relax the muscles in the airways, which can further improve breathing. Furthermore, sweet basil tea has been traditionally used to treat respiratory conditions, including asthma, due to its soothing and calming effects on the respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet basil tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for asthma patients. This is due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
Bronchodilator: The tea can help relax bronchial muscles, easing constriction and improving airflow. This effect is attributed to the presence of caffeic acid and camphor.

Expectorant: Sweet basil tea aids in expelling mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and discomfort in asthma. The expectorant properties come from compounds such as eucalyptol and limonene.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms. The antioxidant activity is provided by rosmarinic acid, lutein, and zeaxanthin.

Calming and Relaxing: Sweet basil tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related asthma symptoms. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils like linalool and citronellol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of essential oils, including eugenol and citronellol, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce airway inflammation and improve breathing for asthma sufferers.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and fatty acids, which have been shown to have bronchodilator and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for managing asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh or dried sweet basil leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the herb to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used basil leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, for added sweetness or tang.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, for best results in managing asthma symptoms.
